_comment: Copy to data/mcp.json (gitignored). Latch is an approval-gating MCP HOST: it connects to these MCP servers (which hold their own credentials in 'env'); the worker never sees them. The worker can only REQUEST a tool call, which becomes an 'mcp_tool_call' approval; the trusted host runs it after you approve. Set MCP_ENABLED=1 or enabled:true to turn it on.

servers.name: filesystem
servers.description: Read/list files under an allowed directory

servers.name: example-with-secret
servers.description: An MCP server that needs an API key (kept on the host, never on the worker)

_fields.transport: 'stdio' spawns the server as a subprocess and speaks JSON-RPC over stdin/stdout; 'mock' is for tests.
_fields.allowedTools: Optional. If non-empty, ONLY these tool names may be called on this server. Empty = allow any tool the server exposes (still approval-gated).
_fields.autoApprove: Optional. Tool names pre-authorised for autonomy auto-approval once bridge-side detection lands. Still audited. Leave empty to require a human for every call.
_fields.argConstraints: Optional per-tool argument bounds enforced host-side before the call runs, for tools that don't self-sandbox. Per field: {"prefix":"..."} | {"equals":value} | {"enum":[...]}. Tool arguments are also validated against the tool's own inputSchema regardless.
